experiment: F-CAT1 FMEA refresh — S377 (26 sessions post-S351)
expect: 2-3 new FMs since S351. NAT predicts >=1 INADEQUATE. New FMs from concurrent staging, knowledge state gaps, or tool archival races.
actual: 5 new FMs (FM-10 through FM-14). 3 INADEQUATE (FM-11 genesis replay, FM-12 fork bomb, FM-14 WSL loose object corruption). NAT recurrence prediction CONFIRMED: predicted FM-10 in ~50 sessions, FM-14 emerged at S364 (13 sessions). L-712 factual error discovered: swarm_colony.py NOT archived — FM-12 defense claim incorrect.
diff: Expected 2-3 new FMs — got 5 (4 from S307 security council now formalized + 1 from S364 incident). Expected >=1 INADEQUATE — got 3. Did NOT predict L-712 factual error or FM-07 operational inertness. NAT timing better than predicted (13s vs 50s prediction).

severity_definitions.1: Catastrophic — permanent data loss or swarm-breaking corruption
severity_definitions.2: Serious — data corruption or major state inconsistency (recoverable)
severity_definitions.3: Moderate — work duplication, minor corruption, auto-recoverable

registry_summary.nat_prediction_status: CONFIRMED — FM-14 emerged at S364 (13 sessions post S351 prediction of 50)
factual_errors_found: L-712: claims swarm_colony.py archived — incorrect, tool is at tools/swarm_colony.py

failure_modes.name: Lesson poisoning via auto-promotion
failure_modes.gap: MINIMAL while evolve.py archived. genesis_evolve.py fitness proxy confounded (L-669).

failure_modes.name: WSL loose object corruption
failure_modes.gap: 0 automated detection. Both layers rule-only. Recovery requires manual reflog manipulation. NAT gray rhino: high probability, known mechanism, zero automated defense.
